*
* Step 1: SU01 — Create test user
*   User: TEST_SALES_01
*   Password: set initial password
*   Roles: ZBR_SALES_PROCESSOR (from PFCG)
*
* Step 2: Login as TEST_SALES_01
*   Open Fiori app, attempt operation
*   Observe: error message or missing button
*
* Step 3: SU53 (as TEST_SALES_01)
*   Transaction: SU53
*   Shows last failed check:
*   Auth Object: Z_SO_AUTH
*   Field      : ACTVT
*   Required   : 01 (create)
*   User has   : 03 (display only)
*
* Step 4: PFCG — Fix role
*   Add ACTVT = 01 to Z_SO_AUTH in ZBR_SALES_PROCESSOR
*   Regenerate profile
*
* Step 5: Re-test with TEST_SALES_01
*   Create button visible — authorization working

rap-bopf-019.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: RAP, BOPF aur Fiori interview mein jawab dene ke baad interviewer agle level ke follow-up questions puchta hai — ye dekhne ke liye ki tumhari knowledge surface-level hai ya deep hai. Common follow-ups hain: 'Managed vs unmanaged RAP kya hai?', 'Custom action kaise add karte ho?', 'Draft handling kya hai?', 'RAP aur BOPF mein kya fark hai?'. Ye questions expect karo aur ready raho. In sab ke short confident answers ready rakhna hi professional dikhata hai.
rap-bopf-019.hinglishMasterExplanation:
RAP, BOPF aur Fiori interview ke 5 most common follow-up question areas hain. Inhe master karo.

Follow-up Area 1: Managed vs Unmanaged RAP
Sabse common follow-up. Managed: 'managed' keyword in BDEF — framework handles DB persistence automatically. Sirf validations, determinations, actions likhte hain. Unmanaged: 'unmanaged' keyword — developer khud FOR MODIFY implement karta hai, BAPI calls karta hai, manually INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE ya BAPI se DB update karta hai. When: managed for greenfield, unmanaged for BAPI wrapping.

Follow-up Area 2: Custom Action Kaise Add Karte Ho
BDEF mein action declare karo: 'action submitOrder result [1] $self;'. Behavior implementation mein method likho: METHOD submitOrder FOR MODIFY IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION SalesOrder~submitOrder RESULT result. Keys se records read karo READ ENTITIES se, business logic apply karo, MODIFY ENTITIES se status update karo, result populate karo.

Follow-up Area 3: Draft Handling Kya Hai
Draft = intermediate save. User form bharna shuru karta hai but complete nahi kiya — data save hona chahiye without creating active record. Draft handling mein: separate draft table, draft lifecycle (create draft > edit > activate/discard). BDEF mein: draft table declare karo, etag field, draft actions (Edit, Activate, Discard, Resume), draft determine action Prepare. Fiori automatically draft save karta hai jab user navigate karta hai.

Follow-up Area 4: RAP vs BOPF Difference
BOPF: deprecated, class-based nodes, manual OData V2 wiring, on-premise only. RAP: CDS-first, BDEF declarative, OData V4 auto-generated, on-premise AND cloud. RAP ne BOPF ko replace kiya — same problem (business object lifecycle) but better architecture.

Follow-up Area 5: CDS Annotations vs BDEF — What's the Difference
CDS annotations: data model annotations — @UI for Fiori layout, @Search for search, @AccessControl for security. BDEF: behavior — operations, validations, actions declare karo. CDS mein field definitions hain. BDEF mein field behaviors hain (mandatory, readonly). Dono complementary hain — CDS = what the entity looks like, BDEF = what it can do.

Bonus Follow-ups:
- 'What is a projection CDS view?' — UI-facing view, @Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER, additional @UI annotations without touching base view
- 'What is a composition in RAP?' — parent-child relationship: header has composition to items
- 'What is REPORTED vs FAILED?' — REPORTED = user message, FAILED = blocks save, both needed
- 'How does Fiori Elements know what to render?' — CDS @UI annotations via OData V4 metadata
rap-bopf-019.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Common follow-up questions from RAP interviews include the managed versus unmanaged distinction — managed means the framework handles database persistence automatically while unmanaged means the developer implements FOR MODIFY and handles all database writes manually, typically when wrapping an existing BAPI. Another frequent follow-up is how to add a custom action — declare it in the BDEF with the action keyword and implement the corresponding FOR MODIFY method in the behavior implementation class. Draft handling questions are also common — draft provides intermediate save capability using a separate draft table, with the framework managing the draft lifecycle through Edit, Activate, Discard, and Resume actions. The RAP versus BOPF comparison comes up frequently — BOPF is deprecated and was class-based with manual OData V2 wiring while RAP is CDS-first, declarative, and auto-generates OData V4 services. Finally, the REPORTED versus FAILED question appears — REPORTED carries the user-visible message while FAILED blocks the save, and both must be populated together in validations.'

Hinglish tip: Follow-up answers ke liye STAR format use karo jahan applicable — Situation, Technical approach, ABAP code reference, Result. Lekin short raho — follow-up answers 2-3 sentences mein deliver karo, long explanation nahi. Interviewer khud aur detail maangega agar chahiye.
rap-bopf-019.codeExamples:
* Common Follow-up Topics — Code Snippets Ready
* ================================================
*
* 1. Custom Action Declaration + Implementation:
define behavior for ZI_SalesOrder alias SalesOrder
{
  action submitOrder result [1] $self;
  action cancelOrder;
}
METHOD submitOrder FOR MODIFY
    IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION SalesOrder~submitOrder
    RESULT result.
  READ ENTITIES OF ZI_SalesOrder ENTITY SalesOrder
    FIELDS ( Status ) WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_orders).
  LOOP AT lt_orders INTO DATA(ls_order).
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_SalesOrder
      ENTITY SalesOrder UPDATE FIELDS ( Status )
      WITH VALUE #( ( %key = ls_order-%key Status = 'SUBMITTED' ) )
      FAILED   DATA(failed)
      REPORTED DATA(reported).
    APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls_order-%key %param = ls_order )
      TO result.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

* 2. Managed vs Unmanaged:
managed implementation ...    " framework handles DB
unmanaged implementation ...  " developer handles DB

* 3. Draft Setup in BDEF:
draft table zdt_sales_order
etag master LastChangedAt
draft action Edit; Activate; Discard; Resume;
draft determine action Prepare;

* 4. REPORTED + FAILED together (always):
APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls-%key ) TO failed-salesorder.
APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls-%key %msg = new_message_with_text(
  severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
  text = 'Validation failed' ) ) TO reported-salesorder.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between managed and unmanaged RAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Managed RAP mein BDEF mein 'managed' likhte hain — framework automatically persistent table mein create, update, delete karta hai. Developer sirf validations, determinations, actions likhta hai. Greenfield entities ke liye best choice. Unmanaged RAP mein 'unmanaged' likhte hain — developer khud FOR MODIFY implement karta hai. BAPI call karo, ya manual SQL likho, ya external API call karo — sab developer ke haath mein. BAPI wrapping ke liye mandatory choice.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In managed RAP, the BDEF keyword 'managed' instructs the framework to automatically handle all database persistence — inserts on create, updates on update, and deletes on delete — using the declared persistent table. Developers implement only validations, determinations, and actions. In unmanaged RAP, 'unmanaged' means the developer must implement the FOR MODIFY method and handle all database operations manually, typically by calling a BAPI or legacy function module. Choose managed for new greenfield entities and unmanaged when wrapping existing BAPIs or legacy write logic.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team decision making moment: new material document feature. Existing BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE already tested, stable, approved. Decision: unmanaged RAP wrapping existing BAPI — all business logic already in BAPI, don't rewrite. Saved 2 weeks of development and avoided regression risk in well-tested BAPI logic.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Managed ko 'always better' samajhna galat hai. Unmanaged is the right choice for BAPI scenarios. Wrong choice mein double writes, lock conflicts, data corruption ho sakti hai.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you add a custom action in RAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Custom action add karne ke 3 steps hain. Step 1: BDEF mein declare karo — 'action submitOrder result [1] $self;'. Instance action hai — specific record pe kaam karta hai. Static action bhi hoti hai — kisi specific record ke bina. Step 2: Behavior implementation class mein method implement karo — METHOD submitOrder FOR MODIFY IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION SalesOrder~submitOrder RESULT result. Step 3: Method mein: keys se records read karo READ ENTITIES se, business logic apply karo, MODIFY ENTITIES se status update karo, result table mein updated records dalo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Adding a custom action in RAP requires three steps. First, declare it in the BDEF — use 'action actionName result [1] $self' for an instance action that operates on specific records, or 'static action actionName' for a global operation. Second, implement the corresponding method in the local handler class with the signature METHOD actionName FOR MODIFY IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION EntityName~actionName RESULT result. Third, in the method body, read the affected records using READ ENTITIES, apply the business logic, update fields using MODIFY ENTITIES, and populate the result parameter with the updated entity instances.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Leave approval action banai thi — manager approval button Fiori mein. BDEF mein: 'action approveLeave result [1] $self'. Method mein: READ ENTITIES se current status check kiya — agar PENDING hai toh MODIFY ENTITIES se APPROVED update kiya, email determination trigger kiya. Fiori mein Approve button automatically appeared because of BDEF action declaration.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Action method mein result populate karna bhool jaana — result [1] $self matlab method result mein current entity state return karna hai. Agar result empty raha toh Fiori app page refresh nahi karega after action.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Step 1: BDEF declaration
action approveLeave result [1] $self;

" Step 2: Method signature (in local handler)
METHOD approveLeave FOR MODIFY
    IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION LeaveRequest~approveLeave
    RESULT result.

" Step 3: Implementation
  READ ENTITIES OF ZI_LeaveRequest
    ENTITY LeaveRequest
    FIELDS ( Status EmployeeId )
    WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_requests).
  LOOP AT lt_requests INTO DATA(ls_req).
    IF ls_req-Status = 'PENDING'.
      MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_LeaveRequest
        ENTITY LeaveRequest
        UPDATE FIELDS ( Status ApprovedBy ApprovedAt )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %key = ls_req-%key
            Status     = 'APPROVED'
            ApprovedBy = sy-uname
            ApprovedAt = utclong_current( ) ) )
        REPORTED DATA(mod_reported).
      APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls_req-%key
          %param = CORRESPONDING #( ls_req ) )
        TO result.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is draft handling in RAP and when is it needed?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Draft handling RAP mein intermediate save capability hai. User ne form bharna shuru kiya, ek field bhari, dusre kaam ke liye gaye — data save rehna chahiye without creating active record. Draft: separate draft table mein save hota hai, active record nahi banta jab tak user explicitly Activate nahi karta. Fiori automatically draft save karta hai — user ko save button press nahi karna padta intermediate state ke liye. Draft kab chahiye: complex multi-step forms mein, long data entry processes mein, collaborative editing scenarios mein jahan multiple users ek record pe kaam kar sakte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Draft handling in RAP provides a save-without-commit capability, allowing users to save intermediate form states without creating active database records. The draft data is stored in a separate draft table. The lifecycle is: user starts editing which creates a draft, makes changes saved automatically by Fiori to the draft table, then explicitly activates the draft which creates or updates the active record, or discards it to abandon changes. Draft handling is needed for complex forms requiring multiple steps or sessions, for preventing data loss during navigation away from incomplete forms, and for locking scenarios where one user editing should prevent others from simultaneously editing the same record.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Purchase order creation app mein user ko 50+ line items enter karne the. Without draft: user enters 30 items, session timeout — sab lost. With draft: automatic save every few seconds, user comes back next day, draft waiting for them. Client specifically requested draft — it was the key selling point of the Fiori app over the old SAP GUI transaction.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Draft table same as active table banana — alag separate table banana padti hai. Draft table mein active table fields ke alawa SAP-specific draft metadata fields hoti hain. Dono tables SE11 mein separately create karo.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does RAP differ from BOPF technically?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: RAP aur BOPF dono business object frameworks hain S/4HANA ke liye — same problem solve karte hain but alag approach se. BOPF (deprecated): ABAP class-based nodes, separate action/validation/determination handler classes, manual OData V2 wiring via SEGW, on-premise only, complex setup. RAP (current): CDS views as foundation, BDEF declarative file for all behaviors, OData V4 auto-generated via service binding, works on-premise AND cloud, simpler setup. Key difference: BOPF code-heavy tha — RAP annotation-driven aur declarative hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: BOPF and RAP both address business object lifecycle management but differ fundamentally in architecture. BOPF used ABAP class-based node definitions with separate handler classes for each concern — action handler, validation handler, determination handler — and required manual OData V2 service wiring through the SEGW Gateway Builder. RAP takes a CDS-first approach where the data model is defined in CDS views, behavior is declared in a single BDEF file covering all operations and validations, and the OData V4 service is automatically generated from the service binding. BOPF was limited to on-premise deployments. RAP works in both on-premise and Public Cloud. BOPF is officially deprecated — SAP recommends RAP for all new development.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Migration project mein BOPF purchase order object RAP mein convert karna tha. BOPF mein: 12 separate class files (node classes, action handlers, validation handlers). RAP mein: 1 BDEF file, 1 behavior implementation class with local handlers. Same functionality, 80% less artifacts to manage. Maintainability dramatically improved.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: BOPF migration ko 1:1 code translation samajhna — architecture alag hai. BOPF node hierarchy RAP composition hierarchy se concept mein similar hai but syntax aur approach completely different. Fresh design karo, copy-paste mat karo.

followupAnswerBank.question: What are static actions vs instance actions in RAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Instance action specific record pe kaam karti hai — BDEF mein sirf 'action' keyword. Example: ek specific order ko submit karo — order ka key chahiye. Static action kisi specific record ke bina kaam karti hai — 'static action' keyword. Example: sab pending orders ek saath process karo — koi specific key nahi chahiye, general operation hai. Fiori mein instance actions Object Page pe dikhte hain (record-specific). Static actions List Report toolbar pe ya global section mein dikhte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Instance actions in RAP operate on specific entity instances — they receive a keys parameter containing the primary keys of the records to act upon. Declared in BDEF as simply 'action actionName'. Used when the operation is record-specific, like approving a particular leave request or submitting a specific sales order. Static actions operate without a specific entity instance — declared as 'static action actionName'. Used for global operations like mass processing all pending approvals or triggering a batch run. In Fiori Elements, instance actions appear on the object page toolbar tied to the displayed record, while static actions appear on the list report toolbar as global operations.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Approval workflow mein dono types use kiye: instance action 'approveLeave' — manager ek specific request approve karta hai Object Page pe. Static action 'massApproveAll' — manager ek button se sab pending requests approve karta hai List Report toolbar se. BDEF mein clearly alag declare kiye, Fiori automatically sahi jagah pe buttons dono ke liye show kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Static action mein keys parameter expect karna — static action mein keys nahi aate, entity-specific nahi hai. Static action mein koi filter chahiye toh method mein READ ENTITIES with own conditions likhna padta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Instance Action — record specific
define behavior for ZI_LeaveRequest
{
  action approveLeave result [1] $self;  " Instance action
  static action massApproveAll;           " Static action
}

" Instance action implementation:
METHOD approveLeave FOR MODIFY
    IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION LeaveRequest~approveLeave
    RESULT result.
  " 'keys' contains specific record keys
  READ ENTITIES OF ZI_LeaveRequest
    ENTITY LeaveRequest FIELDS ( Status )
    WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys ) RESULT DATA(lt_req).
  " Process specific records from keys
ENDMETHOD.

" Static action implementation:
METHOD massApproveAll FOR MODIFY
    IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION LeaveRequest~massApproveAll.
  " No specific keys — find all pending
  READ ENTITIES OF ZI_LeaveRequest
    ENTITY LeaveRequest FIELDS ( Status )
    WITH VALUE #( )  " Empty — read all accessible
    RESULT DATA(lt_all).
  " Filter and approve all PENDING records
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a projection CDS view in RAP and why is it needed?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Projection CDS view RAP ka UI-facing layer hai. Base active entities (ZI_ prefix) data model define karti hain — reusable, stable. Projection entities (ZC_ prefix) UI-specific annotations aur aliases add karti hain without touching base entity. Why separate: ek base entity multiple projections rakh sakti hai — ek Fiori UI ke liye, ek API consumer ke liye, different annotations alag alag. Base entity stable rehti hai — projection badal sakte hain. @Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER annotation projection mein hoti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A projection CDS view in RAP is the UI-facing or consumer-facing layer that sits on top of the base active entity. Base entities, typically prefixed with ZI, define the stable data model with associations and basic annotations. Projection entities, typically prefixed with ZC, add consumer-specific @UI annotations, field aliases, and metadata layer specifications using @Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER. The separation allows one base entity to serve multiple consumers through different projections — a Fiori Elements projection with full UI annotations and an external API projection exposing only relevant fields without UI annotations. The service definition exposes the projection entities, not the base entities.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Same Sales Order base entity — two projections: ZC_SalesOrder_UI for Fiori with all @UI annotations, criticality, header info. ZC_SalesOrder_API for external system integration — no UI annotations, different field aliases matching the integration contract. Base entity ZI_SalesOrder unchanged. Two consumers, one data model, clean separation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Service definition mein base entity (ZI_) expose karna — galat. Always expose projection entity (ZC_) in service definition. Base entities directly exposed karne se UI annotations miss hoti hain aur metadata layer concept break hota hai.

followupAnswerBank.question: What follow-up questions come from explaining validations in RAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Validation explain karne ke baad common follow-ups: 'REPORTED aur FAILED dono kab chahiye?' — hamesha dono saath chahiye validation mein. FAILED blocks save, REPORTED shows message. 'Validation on save vs on modify kya hai?' — on save: save button press pe trigger. on modify: field change hone pe immediate trigger. 'READ ENTITIES kyun use karo SELECT ki jagah?' — RAP transactional buffer se latest data milta hai, SELECT DB se stale data deta hai. 'Field-level validation kaise karo?' — BDEF mein field name specify karo validation ke saath.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Common follow-up questions after explaining validations include: why must both REPORTED and FAILED be populated — REPORTED provides the user-visible message while FAILED signals the framework to block the save, and omitting either produces incorrect behavior. What is the difference between on save and on modify triggers — on save fires at the save event after all user input is complete, while on modify fires immediately when specified fields change, useful for real-time feedback. Why use READ ENTITIES instead of SELECT — SELECT reads the database and misses uncommitted in-buffer changes while READ ENTITIES reads the transactional buffer ensuring the latest in-flight state. How to trigger a validation for specific fields only — declare the field list in the BDEF: validation checkDates on save { field OrderDate, DeliveryDate; }.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Interview mein validation explain kiya — interviewer ne turant pucha: 'Ab batao agar FAILED miss karein toh kya hoga?' Prepared tha: 'Record save ho jaayega despite error message showing.' Interviewer satisfied. Phir: 'on save vs on modify?' Prepared tha: 'on save for final validation, on modify for real-time feedback.' Two follow-ups, both answered confidently.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: on modify trigger ka overuse — har field pe on modify validation lagana performance hit deta hai. on save prefer karo validations ke liye unless real-time feedback specifically required hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Validation with field-level trigger
validation checkDates on save
  { field OrderDate, DeliveryDate; }  " Only these fields trigger it

" Both REPORTED + FAILED (mandatory pattern)
METHOD checkDates FOR VALIDATE
    IMPORTING keys FOR SalesOrder~checkDates.
  READ ENTITIES OF ZI_SalesOrder  " Buffer, not DB
    ENTITY SalesOrder
    FIELDS ( OrderDate DeliveryDate )
    WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_so).
  LOOP AT lt_so INTO DATA(ls_so).
    IF ls_so-DeliveryDate < ls_so-OrderDate.
      " FAILED — blocks save
      APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls_so-%key )
        TO failed-salesorder.
      " REPORTED — shows message
      APPEND VALUE #(
        %key = ls_so-%key
        %msg = new_message_with_text(
          severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
          text = 'Delivery date must be after order date' ) )
        TO reported-salesorder.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What follow-up questions come from explaining Fiori Elements and RAP integration?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Fiori Elements explain karne ke baad common follow-ups: 'CDS annotation syntax kya hai @UI.lineItem ke liye?' — array format: [{ position: 10 }] — square brackets mandatory. '@UI.facets kya hai?' — Object Page mein sections define karta hai, groupings, reference types. 'Custom extension point kaise add karte hain?' — Controller Extension, Column Fragment, Additional Fields — Extension Points use karo without forking. 'Fiori app Service Binding se kaise connect hoti hai?' — manifest.json mein OData service URL reference karo. 'Criticality color coding kaise karo?' — integer field reference karo: 0=grey, 1=red, 2=orange, 3=green.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: After explaining Fiori Elements and RAP integration, common follow-ups include: the correct syntax for @UI.lineItem — always an array with square brackets even for a single entry. What @UI.facets does — it defines the sections on the Object Page, specifying which identification group or child entity table appears in each facet with position and label. How to add a custom extension without replacing the entire Fiori app — use Fiori Elements Extension Points such as controller extensions or custom column fragments. How the Fiori app knows the OData endpoint — the manifest.json app descriptor references the service binding URL in its dataSources section. How criticality color coding works — reference an integer field in the entity where 0 is grey, 1 is red, 2 is orange, and 3 is green.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: UAT mein client ne extra column maanga — 'Due Date' column List Report mein. Fiori app custom UI5 se banaya nahi tha — Fiori Elements tha. Solution: CDS projection mein @UI.lineItem annotation add kiya DueDate field pe, service binding re-published. Without writing any code, column appeared. Client: 'Ye toh bahut fast tha!' Fiori Elements ki power demonstrate hua.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Criticality ke liye string field reference karna — ye kaam nahi karta. Criticality sirf integer field se work karta hai (0/1/2/3). String field se criticality map karni ho toh CASE statement use karo CDS mein integer derive karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Fiori Elements Integration Follow-up Code:

" 1. @UI.lineItem array syntax (mandatory)
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10, label: 'Order ID' }]  " Correct
@UI.lineItem: { position: 10 }                        " WRONG - no []

" 2. @UI.facets for Object Page sections
@UI.facet: [
  { id: 'Header',
    type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
    label: 'Order Details',
    position: 10 },
  { id: 'Items',
    type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
    label: 'Line Items',
    position: 20,
    targetElement: '_Items' }
]

" 3. Criticality (integer field required)
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 30, criticality: 'StatusColor' }]
status      as Status,
CASE status
  WHEN 'NEW'       THEN 0  " grey
  WHEN 'PENDING'   THEN 2  " orange  
  WHEN 'APPROVED'  THEN 3  " green
  WHEN 'REJECTED'  THEN 1  " red
END                as StatusColor  " Integer field for criticality
